rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. BETTY YEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Entered On January 19, 2019. (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 2, Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Entered On January 19, 2019. (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn). The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans' motion for reconsideration of the order denying its motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Assuming without deciding that the motion is permitted by CCP 1008 or, if not, is a proper invitation for the court to exercise its authority per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 to reconsider the prior order which the court would accept, Thrivent has failed to show that the judgment invalidating the "underground" regulations conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons. The salient portions of the Westmark declaration are inadmissible hearsay. The salient portions of the Lovendusky declaration lack foundation, are conclusions of law and/or are speculation. The salient portions or the declaration sought to be sealed, even if considered, fail to show that the declarant's three companies received a significant benefit from the judgment in this case because of the practices that those companies had in place before the judgment and, even if those companies did receive a significant benefit from the judgment, the three companies do not constitute a "large class of persons" within the meaning of CCP 1021.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Controller is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. BETTY YEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Certain Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' Motion To Seal Certain Documents. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans' motion to seal certain documents is denied. The motion to seal papers and the declaration sought to be sealed fail to make the required "specific showing of serious injury" if the declaration sought to be sealed is made available to the public. (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal. App. 4th 1273, 1282). "In delineating the injury to be prevented, specificity is essential. Broad allegations of harm, bereft of specific examples of articulated reasoning, are insufficient." (Id. quoting In re Cendent (3rd Cir. 2001) 260 F.3d 183, 194). Moreover, because the declaration sought to be sealed is offered to show that a significant benefit has been conferred on a large class of persons in order to obtain an order requiring a governmental agency to pay a substantial amount of fees, there is a heightened right of public access for all materials on which the court might rely in ruling on the motion. Due to the concerns expressed by the declarant of the declaration sought to be sealed, the court is amenable to Thrivent's withdrawing the declaration rather than having it made available to the public and, in such event, the court's ruling on the motion for reconsideration would not include any reference to the declaration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Controller is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT NORTHERN PACIFIC ROOFING, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Cross-defendant Northern Pacific Roofing, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication as to the causes of action stated against it by cross-complainants Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC in their first amended cross-complaint is granted as to all causes of action. Northern Pacific Roofing is entitled to summary adjudication of the causes of action for equitable indemnity/ implied contractual indemnity, contribution, and negligence because these actions are extinguished by the Court's December 20, 2018 order granting a determination of a good faith settlement between Northern Pacific Roofing and Plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association. (Code Civ. Proc. § 877.6(c).) The negligence cause of action is also extinguished by the order, both because the order left remaining only the contractual causes of action and because the negligence cause of action is a disguised claim for equitable indemnity. (See Gackstetter v. Frawley (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1257, 1274 ["a party may not avoid a section 877.6 motion by providing different labels for what are in reality indemnity or contribution claims."]; The Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Construction Management, Inc. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 595, 607 [agreeing that project architect's negligence claim against construction managers "is essentially a claim for indemnification and an attempt to avoid the consequences of the good faith settlement. To permit [architect's] claim would subvert California's public policy of encouraging good faith settlement."). Summary adjudication is granted on the express indemnity and breach of contract causes of action because the undisputed facts show that there was no contract between the parties. The subcontract between Northern Pacific Roofing and MBC does not impose any obligation on Northern Pacific Roofing to indemnify and defend Fifth Historic, but only to name it as an additional insured on its workers' compensatioon and general liability insurance policies, which Northern Pacific Roofing did. (McNerney Decl. 3-4, Exh. 1 at NPR000428, NPR000439.) The motion for summary adjudication of the express and implied warranty cause of action is moot, because the cross-complainants dismissed that cause of action in the operative first amended complaint filed on February 22, 2019, after Northern Pacific Roofing moved for summary judgment or adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Northern Pacific Roofing is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SAFEDOCS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. SWETT & CRAWFORD A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents From Sweit And Crawford
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS SAFEDOCS, INC., TERRY BIBBER, SHIRLEY BIBBER, RUSS DAVIS, AND KURT NEUMANN'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents From Swett And Crawford Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court wishes to disclose to the parties that Judge Pro Tem Julia Campins has a personal relationship with Craig Pinedo, counsel for Plaintiff. Their children attend the same school and are friends. The Court does not believe this is a basis for disqualification and will be impartial with respect to this matter. The Court notes that Swett & Crawford complains that the Motion is both late-filed and "rash." The Court holds that the motion is timely, pursuant to CCP § 2024.020, and that Safedocs sufficiently met and conferred, considering the circumstances. The Court grants the Motion in part and denies it in part. Requests 1 and 2: Swett & Crawford has not demonstrated a sufficient basis for withholding documents. It must produce an appropriate privilege log for all documents withheld on the basis of privilege. All other bases for withholding are not appropriate here. Requests 4 and 5: It appears that Safedocs has clarified the meaning of the terms in the Request and Swett & Crawford continues to declare that no such responsive documents exist. To the extent Swett & Crawford is properly interpreting the request, the Motion is denied. Requests 6 and 7: Swett & Crawford must utilize a reasonable interpretation of the terms of the Request and produce responsive documents. Request 8: The Court agrees that facts are not privileged. If any true opinions are contained in the notes, then Swett & Crawford may redact those opinions. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE LAPAYOWKER VS. AIRBNB,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Reconsider The Court'S Rulings On April 8, 2019 And Alternatively Motion For Relief Under Ccp Sec 473
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LESLIE LAPAYOWKER's Motion To Reconsider The Court'S Rulings On April 8, 2019 And Alternatively Motion For Relief Under Ccp Sec 473. Plaintiff Leslie Lapayowker's motion to reconsider the Court's April 8, 2019 orders and alternative motion for relief under Code Civ. Proc. § 473 is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that any "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" warrant reconsideration of the Court's rulings. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1008(a).) On March 11, 2019, Defendant timely filed and served its motions for an order adopting the tentative ruling of the judge pro tem on Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and for an order denying Plaintiff's motion for protective order. Both motions were set for hearing on April 8, 2019. Plaintiff did not file any opposition to either motion. Even if that was an excusable oversight that in light of the prior default and any confusion relating to the posting of the judge pro tem's tentative ruling, Plaintiff does not offer any reason for failing to contest the Court's tentative rulings posted on April 7, 2019, or for failing to appear at the hearing on April 8. Plaintiff therefore has failed to show any basis for relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW DEL VALLE VS. MENZIES AVIATION INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion To Approve Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW DEL VALLE's Motion To Approve Paga Settlement. Plaintiff Matthew DelValle's motion for approval of PAGA settlement is denied in part without prejudice. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code § 2699(l)(2); see Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-1135.) However, Plaintiff has failed to carry his burden to show that the amount of attorney's fees and costs sought is reasonable. (Lab. Code § 2699(g)(1).) Accordingly, the motion is denied without prejudice to Plaintiff's counsel making an additional information sufficient to carry that burden. Plaintiff seeks an award of $2,150,000 in attorneys' fees and costs, 40% of the gross settlement amount. Although the standard for an award of attorneys' fees under PAGA has not been clearly established, the usual method for calculating an attorneys' fees award under a statutory fee-shifting provision is the lodestar approach. (Laffitte v. Robert Half Int'l Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480, 500.) The Court acknowledges that in the analogous context of class action litigation, an attorneys' fees award is not per se unreasonable merely because it is calculated as a percentage of a common fund preserved or recovered by means of litigation; however, "[t]he choice of a fee calculation method is generally one within the discretion of the trial court, the goal under either the percentage or lodestar approach being the award of a reasonable fee to compensate counsel for their efforts." (Id. at 504.) Further, even if a court selects the percentage fee, it generally will double-check the reasonableness of the percentage fee through a lodestar calculation, which "provides a mechanism for bringing an objective measure of the work performed into the calculation of a reasonable attorney fee." (Id. at 488, 504.) Here, the Court intends to utilize both methods. Because Plaintiff has provided only limited information regarding the tasks performed by counsel during the litigation, and no information whatever regarding the number of hours worked, the hourly rates sought for that work, the reasonableness of those rates, or the overall lodestar, the Court cannot determine whether the fee award sought is reasonable. Accordingly, the motion is denied without prejudice to a further submission. Plaintiff should be prepared at the hearing to propose a date by which that filing will be made and a continued hearing date on the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MINOO PARSAZADEH VS. HAMPTON HEALTH, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Quash Subpoenas For Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF MINOO PARSAZADEH'S Motion For Order To Quash Subpoenas For Business Records Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court is inclined to grant the Motion with respect to Lyft, Senior Services Group, 1700 California St. Associates, Fall Prevention and Dizziness Therapy, Minoo Parsa, and Ragghianti Freitas LLP. The Court requires further information regarding the relevance of the subpoenas and possibilities for limitations as to Bank of America and Dr. Moghaddam. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Motiva Enterprises Llc; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT MAXUM INDEMNITY COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Motiva Enterprises LLC Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Many of Motiva's objections are boilerplate and disfavored. Nevertheless, some of the discovery requests are problematic. Requests for Production 5, 6, and 7: Granted. Requests for Production 13, 18, and 28: The Court believes a stipulation would relieve the conflict. Request for Production 17 and Special Interrogatory No. 8; Denied because they lack reasonable particularity. Request for Production No. 24: Granted, as it appears relevant and Motiva has failed to articulate a burden. Requests for Production 23, 26, and 27: Denied. The Court cannot see the relevance aside from broad statements about possibilities that are unexplained at this stage of the litigation. Form Interrogatory No. 17.1: Granted to the extent Motiva failed to identify responsive persons and documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAMER NASSER ET AL VS. LISA MICHELLE MCCORMICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 6, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Lisa Michelle McCormick, Estate of Robert McCormick, Steely McCormick, Paul Charles Steely, and Michelle McCormick's demurrer to Plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff's first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have not properly pled negligent entrustment because such a claim requires pleading of facts that the owner of the vehicle entrusted the vehicle to someone known to be unfit to drive. (Lindstrom v. Hertz Corp. (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 644, 648.) The first amended complaint only alleges facts related to failing to secure the car and allowing it to be stolen. As a matter of law, this does not support a claim for negligent entrustment. Plaintiffs have leave to amend if they can allege in good faith that negligent entrustment occurred voluntarily with knowledge or reason to know of the driver's unfitness to drive. With regard to the remainder of the first amended complaint, Plaintiffs may plead inconsistent allegations in the alternative. (Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Plaintiffs' responses to interrogatories are not dispositive regarding who they may plead to be the owner and driver of the vehicle at this stage of the proceedings. (Mason v. Marriage & Family Center (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 537, 546.) However, the Court urges Plaintiffs to concisely and clearly state which of the five named Defendants are alleged in the alternative to be the driver of the vehicle and which are alleged in the alternative to own the vehicle and to have negligently entrusted the vehicle to the driver. In addition, if Plaintiffs seek to plead a separate cause of action for negligent entrustment, that cause of action should be pled separately from any claim of negligence. (See Cal. R. Ct. 2.112.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
